channel.title: Expanding Land — Updates & Changelog
channel.description: Latest updates, new features, bug fixes, server improvements, and community content for Expanding Land — the free IO game with 1024 players per arena and zero bots. Built entirely in C. Compatible with Legend Mod, Delta, and Agar2.

item.title: IO Games Glossary — 40+ Terms Every Player Should Know
item.description:
Complete dictionary of IO game terms: splitting, tricksplit, popsplit, virus, W, mass, multiboxing, teaming, anti-team, and more. Learn the language of Agar.io, Slither.io, and Expanding Land.

Includes more than 40 terms fully defined and sorted into categories.

Read the full Glossary

item.title: Best IO Games in 2026 — Top 15 Free Browser Games Ranked
item.description:
The definitive ranked list of the best free IO games you can play in your browser in 2026. Featuring 15 games including Agar.io, Slither.io, Diep.io, Surviv.io, Krunker.io, and Expanding Land — with honest reviews, player counts, and a side-by-side comparison table.

Includes a quick comparison of the top 5 cell/growth games by max players, bot status, map type, and cost.

item.title: How to Get Good at IO Games — Complete Strategy Guide
item.description:
A comprehensive strategy guide for cell IO games. Covers everything from basic controls to advanced techniques:

  • Controls & basics — mouse movement, splitting, ejecting mass
  • Early game survival — eating dots, avoiding bigger cells, staying near edges
  • Splitting — the 2× rule, when to split vs. wait
  • Virus mechanics — defensive hiding, offensive virus shooting (7 W presses)
  • Mass management — decay rates, strategic ejecting
  • Map positioning — corners vs. edges vs. center
  • Advanced techniques — tricksplit, popsplit, bait-and-switch
  • Expanding Land-specific tips — dynamic map, 1024 players, spectator learning

item.title: Why No Bots? — How Expanding Land Keeps Every Player Real
item.description:
Why does Expanding Land ban all bots? This article explains the strict no-bots policy and why real-player-only gameplay creates better competition:

  • The bot problem in modern IO games — fake players, hollow leaderboards
  • Every cell in Expanding Land is controlled by a real human
  • Real competition means every kill is a real outplay
  • 1024 player capacity means arenas fill naturally without needing bots
  • Dynamic map shrinking keeps matches dense even with fewer players
  • Multiboxing allowed (up to 4 accounts) as a skill-based alternative to bots

item.title: Agar.io vs. Expanding Land — Side-by-Side Comparison
item.description:
A factual side-by-side comparison between Agar.io (launched 2015) and Expanding Land (launched 2026). Covers:

  • Max players — 1024 (EL) vs 200 (Agar.io)
  • Bots — none in EL, prevalent in Agar.io
  • Map — dynamic ¼× to 4× scaling (EL) vs fixed size (Agar.io)
  • Server language — Pure C (EL) vs C++ (Agar.io)
  • Multiboxing — regulated 4 accounts (EL) vs unregulated (Agar.io)
  • Mod support — Legend Mod, Delta, and Agar2 all compatible
  • Price — 100% free with no pay-to-win

item.title: April 2026 — Live Server Status, Anti-Flicker Spectate Fix & Map Scale Display
item.description:
New Features:

  • Live server status endpoint: GET /status returns real-time JSON with player count, spectators, uptime, and current map scale
  • LegendMod homepage and Help Center now show live ONLINE/OFFLINE badge with dynamic player counts fetched from the game server
  • Map scale indicator displays current map tier (1/4, 1/2, 1×, 2×, up to 8×) in real time
  • Status updates run on the accept thread with lock-free volatile reads — zero impact on game loop performance

Bug Fixes:

  • Fixed cell flickering/blinking during spectate mode — increased MAX_QUERY_NODES from 8192 to 65536 (8×) to prevent non-deterministic spatial query truncation
  • view_state now properly cleared on every spectate mode transition, fixing one-frame blinks caused by stale add/update classification
  • Full-map spectate was already immune (bypasses spatial queries) — grouped, center, and free-roam modes are now stable too

item.title: April 2026 — Viewport Culling, Session Isolation & OOM Protection
item.description:
Performance & Stability:

  • MAX_VIEW_NODES limit set to 16384 — prevents out-of-memory crashes during extreme zoom-out while keeping 30× headroom for spectators viewing crowded arenas
  • Viewport culling now sends only nodes visible in the player's current view — eliminates FPS drops when zoomed out on large maps with many cells

Session Management:

  • Conditional logout: client auto-signs out when joining a private server that isn't Expanding Land, preventing opcode 102 conflicts that caused disconnects
  • Login buttons (Facebook, Google, Discord) are now disabled on server types that don't support them, preventing confusing error states

Bug Fixes:

  • Fixed cell color inconsistencies under high player density by including name and skin data in every update packet

item.title: March 2026 — Cell Merging Restored, Mobile Controls Fixed
item.description:
Server-Side Fixes:

  • Cell merging logic restored and stabilized — merging now works correctly under high-density scenarios where many cells overlap
  • Player identity consistency improved: name and skin data included in every cell update packet, fixing cases where cells appeared with wrong colors

Client-Side Improvements:

  • Mobile touch controls: "Stop Where Finger Was" (Target Mode) fully restored — cell stops immediately on finger release when enabled
  • Multibox auto-spectate fix ported from jim branch to the TypeScript branch
  • Full Map spectate button now correctly triggers legendmod.sendAction(56) exclusively on Expanding Land servers

item.title: March 2026 — Login Disconnect Fix for Legacy Servers
item.description:
Login Security:

  • Fixed game disconnects that occurred on imsolo and agar2 servers when logging in via Google or Facebook authentication
  • Login opcode 102 (_lw_sendLogin102) is now conditionally skipped for legacy server types that don't support it, while remaining active for Expanding Land
  • Encrypted WebSocket (WSS/TLS) connections enforced across all server types for secure communication

item.title: Core Features — Expanding Land Server Engine & Gameplay
item.description:
Server Architecture:

  • Entire server written from scratch in pure C
  • 1024 concurrent real players per arena — zero bots, zero AI opponents
  • Pre-allocated memory pools, split quadtree optimization for efficient spatial queries
  • Server located in Nuremberg, Germany (Hetzner) for EU-optimized latency

Gameplay Features:

  • Resizable map system: ¼× to 4× dynamic scaling based on active player count
  • 200+ spectator slots with free-roam camera
  • Anti-teaming system using discrete event-based penalty scoring — each action (virus, split, W, danger zone) tracked independently with its own lifespan
  • Ghost cells on death — see exactly where you died for learning from mistakes
  • Session stats: time alive, highest mass, food eaten, players eaten
  • In-game chat system

Compatibility & Access:

  • Full compatibility with Legend Mod, Delta, and Agar2 browser extensions
  • Android APK available (standalone app via WebView wrapper, no Play Store needed)
  • Discord OAuth login integration
  • Strict no-bots policy — automated play detected and blocked
  • Up to 4 multibox accounts per player (regulated, not unlimited)
